Annual Exam - 2022

Code: 65/1/3 (set-3)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 8 printed pages.
  • Q.P. Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 14 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  • 15 minutes time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:40

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them :

  1. The question paper contains three Sections - Section A, B and C.
  2. Each Section is Compulsory.
  3. Section - A has 6 short answer type-I questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section - B has 4 short answer type-II questions of 3 marks each.
  5. Section - C has 4 long answer type questions of 4 marks each.
  6. There is an internal choice in some questions.
  7. Question No. 14 is a case based problem with 2 subparts of 2 marks each.

SECTION - A
Question numbers 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each.
1.

Let A and B be two events such that P(A) = {tex}\frac{5}{8}{/tex}, P(B) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} and P(A/B) = {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}. Find the value of P(B/A).

 2
2.

Two balls are drawn at random from a bag containing 2 red balls and 3 blue balls, without replacement. Let the variable X denotes the number of red balls. Find the probability distribution of X.

 2
3.

Find the sum of the order and the degree of the differential equation: {tex}\left(x+\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}\right)^2=\left(\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}\right)^2+1{/tex}

 2
4.(i)

If {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{d} x}[\mathrm{~F}(x)]=\frac{\sec ^4 x}{\operatorname{cosec}^4 x}{/tex} and {tex}\mathrm{F}\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}, then find F(x).

 2

OR
(ii)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{\log x-3}{(\log x)^4} \mathrm{~d} x{/tex}.
5.

Find the values of {tex}\lambda{/tex}, for which the distance of point (2, 1, {tex}\lambda{/tex}) from plane 3x + 5y + 4z = 11 is {tex}2\sqrt{2}{/tex} units.

 2
6.

If {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}+y \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} are two vectors for which the vector {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}){/tex} is perpendicular to the vector {tex}(\vec{a}-\vec{b}){/tex}, then find all the possible values of y.

 2

SECTION - B
Question numbers 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each.
7.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{\mathrm{d} x}{\sqrt{x}+\sqrt[3]{x}}{/tex}.

 3

OR
(ii)

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos x}{(1+\sin x)(4+\sin x)} \mathrm{d} x{/tex}.
8.

Find the co-ordinates of point where the line {tex}\frac{x-3}{-1}=\frac{y+4}{1}=\frac{z+5}{6}{/tex} crosses the plane passing through points {tex}\left(\frac{7}{2}, 0,0\right){/tex}, (0, 7, 0) and (0, 0, 7).

 3
9.

Solve the differential equation: {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}=\frac{x^2+y^2}{x y}{/tex}.

 3
10.(i)

If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{d}{/tex} are four non-zero vectors such that {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{c} \times \vec{d}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{c}=4 \vec{b} \times \vec{d}{/tex}, then show that {tex}(\vec{a}-2 \vec{d}){/tex} is parallel to {tex}(2 \vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} where {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \neq 2 \vec{\mathrm{d}}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{\mathrm{c}} \neq 2 \vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex}.

 3

OR
(ii)

The two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are {tex}2 \hat { i } - 4 \hat { j } - 5 \hat { k }{/tex}and {tex}2 \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k }.{/tex} Find the two unit vectors parallel to its diagonals. Using the diagonal vectors, find the area of the parallelogram.

SECTION - C
Question numbers 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each.
11.

A card from a pack of 52 playing cards is lost. From the remaining cards, 2 cards are drawn at random without replacement and are found to be both aces. Find the probability that lost card being an ace.

 4
12.

Evaluate {tex} \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x } { 1 + \sin x } d x.{/tex}

 4
13.

Using integration, find the area of the region enclosed by the curve y = x2, the x-axis and the ordinates x = -2 and x = 1.

 4

OR

Using integration, find the area of the region enclosed by line y = {tex}\sqrt{3}x{/tex}, semi-circle y = {tex}\sqrt{4-x^2}{/tex} and x-axis in first quadrant.

CASE BASED / DATA BASED QUESTION
14.

Electrical transmission wires which are laid down in winters are stretched tightly to accommodate expansion in summers.

Two such wires lie along the following lines:
l1 : {tex}\frac{x+1}{3}=\frac{\mathrm{y}-3}{-2}=\frac{\mathrm{z}+2}{-1}{/tex}
l2 : {tex}\frac{x}{-1}=\frac{\mathrm{y}-7}{3}=\frac{\mathrm{z}+7}{-2}{/tex}

Based on the given information, answer the following questions:

(i)

Are the lines l1 and l2 coplanar? Justify your answer.

 2
(ii)

Find the point of intersection of the lines l1 and l2.

 2

Code: 65/1/3 (set-3)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 8 printed pages.
  • Q.P. Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 14 questions.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:40

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them :

  1. The question paper contains three Sections - Section A, B and C.
  2. Each Section is Compulsory.
  3. Section - A has 6 short answer type-I questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section - B has 4 short answer type-II questions of 3 marks each.
  5. Section - C has 4 long answer type questions of 4 marks each.
  6. There is an internal choice in some questions.
  7. Question No. 14 is a case based problem with 2 subparts of 2 marks each.

SECTION - A

Question numbers 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each.
1.

Let A and B be two events such that P(A) = {tex}\frac{5}{8}{/tex}, P(B) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} and P(A/B) = {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}. Find the value of P(B/A).

Solution:

∵ P(A) = {tex}\frac58{/tex}​​​​​​, P(B) = {tex}\frac12{/tex} , P(A/B) = {tex}\frac34{/tex}
∵ {tex}P\left(\frac{A}{B}\right)=\frac{P(A \cap B)}{P(B)}{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}\frac34=\frac{P(A\cap B)}{\frac12}{/tex}​
​​​​​{tex}P(A\cap B)=\frac38{/tex}
Now, {tex}P\left(\frac{B}{A}\right)=\frac{P(B \cap A)}{P(A)}{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}P\left(\frac{B}{A}\right)=\frac{\frac{3}{8}}{\frac{5}{8}}=\frac{3}{5}{/tex}​​​​​​​

 2
2.

Two balls are drawn at random from a bag containing 2 red balls and 3 blue balls, without replacement. Let the variable X denotes the number of red balls. Find the probability distribution of X.

Solution:

Given, the number of red balls in the bag = 2
and, the number of blue balls in the bag = 3
Total number of balls in the bag = 2 + 3 = 5
Given, two balls are drawn at random without replacement.
X denotes the number of red balls and ‘X’ can be 0, 1 and 2.
When no red ball is drawn from the bag, X = 0,
P (X = 0) = {tex}\frac35\times\frac24=\frac3{10}{/tex}
When one red ball is drawn from the bag, X = 1 ,
P(X = 1) = P(RB) + P(BR) = {tex}\frac25\times\frac14+\frac35\times\frac24{/tex}
P(X = 1) = {tex}\frac2{20}+\frac6{20}=\frac8{20}=\frac25{/tex}​​​​​​​
When both balls are red X = 2,
P(X = 2) = {tex}\frac25\times\frac14=\frac1{10}{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The probability distribution of X

X 0 1 2
f(X) {tex}\frac3{10}{/tex} {tex}\frac25{/tex} {tex}\frac1{10}{/tex}
2
3.

Find the sum of the order and the degree of the differential equation: {tex}\left(x+\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}\right)^2=\left(\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}\right)^2+1{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\because{/tex} highest derivative is {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex}. so order is 1.
And degree is highest power of highest derivative so degree is 2.

 2
4.(i)

If {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{d} x}[\mathrm{~F}(x)]=\frac{\sec ^4 x}{\operatorname{cosec}^4 x}{/tex} and {tex}\mathrm{F}\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=\frac{\pi}{4}{/tex}, then find F(x).

Solution:

F(x) = {tex}\int \tan ^4 x d x=\int \tan ^2 x \cdot\left(\sec ^2 x-1\right) d x{/tex}
{tex}=\int\left(\tan ^2 x \sec ^2 x-\sec ^2 x+1\right) d x{/tex}
F(x) = {tex}\frac{\tan ^3 x}{3}-\tan x+x+C{/tex}
x = {tex}\frac {\pi}{4}{/tex}, F(x) = {tex}\frac {\pi}{4}{/tex} gives C = {tex}\frac {2}{3}{/tex}
F(x) = {tex}\frac{\tan ^3 x}{3}-\tan x+x+\frac{2}{3}{/tex}

 2

OR
(ii)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{\log x-3}{(\log x)^4} \mathrm{~d} x{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}I=\int \frac{\log x-3}{(\log x)^4} \mathrm{~d} x{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\frac{\log x}{(\log x)^4}dx-\int\frac3{\left(\log x\right)^4}dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\frac1{(\log x)^3}dx-\int\frac3{\left(\log x\right)^4}dx{/tex} ...(i)
Taking {tex}I_1=\int\frac1{(\log x)^3}.1dx{/tex}
Using integration by parts
{tex}I_1=\frac1{\left(\log x\right)^3}.x-\int\frac{\left(-3\right)}{x(\log x)^4}.xdx{/tex}
{tex}I_1=\frac x{\left(\log x\right)^3}+\int\frac3{(\log x)^4}dx{/tex}
Putting in eqn (i)
{tex}I=\frac x{\left(\log x\right)^3}+\int\frac3{(\log x)^4}dx-\int\frac3{\left(\log x\right)^4}dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac x{\left(\log x\right)^3}{/tex}

 2
5.

Find the values of {tex}\lambda{/tex}, for which the distance of point (2, 1, {tex}\lambda{/tex}) from plane 3x + 5y + 4z = 11 is {tex}2\sqrt{2}{/tex} units.

Solution:

{tex}\because{/tex} given point is (2, 1, {tex}\lambda{/tex} ) and equation of plane is 3x + 5y + 4z = 11
Also distance between them is {tex}2\sqrt2{/tex} unit
distance {tex}=\left|\frac{Ax_1+By_1+Cz_1-d}{\sqrt{A^2+B^2+C^2}}\right|{/tex}
Here A = 3, B = 5, C = 4, D = 11, x1 = 2, y1 = 1, z1 = {tex}\lambda{/tex}
{tex}2\sqrt2=\left|\frac{3\times2+5\times1+4\times\lambda-11}{\sqrt{3^2+5^2+4^2}}\right|{/tex}
{tex}2\sqrt2=\left|\frac{6+5+4\lambda-11}{\sqrt{9+25+16}}\right|{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}2\sqrt2=\left|\frac{4\lambda}{\sqrt{50}}\right|{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}2 \sqrt{2} \times 5 \sqrt{2}{/tex} = 4{tex}\lambda{/tex}​​​​​​​
4{tex}\lambda{/tex} = 20​​​​​​​
{tex}\lambda{/tex} = 5​​​​​​​

 2
6.

If {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}+y \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} are two vectors for which the vector {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}){/tex} is perpendicular to the vector {tex}(\vec{a}-\vec{b}){/tex}, then find all the possible values of y.

Solution:

{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow a=2\widehat i+y\widehat j+\widehat k{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow b=\widehat i+2\widehat j+3\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b=3\widehat i+\left(y+2\right)\widehat j+4\widehat k\;{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow a-\overrightarrow b=\widehat i+\left(y-2\right)\widehat j-2\widehat k\;{/tex}
Now as they both are perpendicular to each other
So, {tex}\left(\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b\right).\left(\overrightarrow a-\overrightarrow b\right)=0{/tex}
3 + (y + 2)(y - 2) - 8 = 0
y2 - 4 - 5 = 0
y2 = 9
y = {tex}\pm3{/tex}

 2

SECTION - B

Question numbers 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each.
7.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{\mathrm{d} x}{\sqrt{x}+\sqrt[3]{x}}{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}I=\int\frac{dx}{\sqrt x+\sqrt[3]x}=\int\frac{dx}{x^\frac12+x^\frac32}{/tex}
Put x = t6 , dx = 6t5 dt
{tex}I=\int\frac{6t^5dt}{t^3+t^2}=6\int\frac{t^3dt}{t+1}{/tex}
{tex}I=6\int\left[t^2-t+1-\frac1{t+1}\right]dt{/tex}
{tex}I=6\left[\frac{t^3}3-\frac{t^2}2+t-\log\left(t+1\right)\right]+C{/tex}
{tex}I=6\left[\frac{\sqrt x}3-\frac{\sqrt[3]x}2+x^{\textstyle\frac16}-\log\left(x^{\textstyle\frac16}+1\right)\right]+c{/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos x}{(1+\sin x)(4+\sin x)} \mathrm{d} x{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}I=\int \limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{\cos x}{(1+\sin x)(4+\sin x)} \mathrm{d} x{/tex}
Put sinx = t
cosx dx = dt
At x = 0 t = 0, at x = {tex}\frac{tex}\pi{/tex}2{/tex} t = 1
{tex}I=\int_\limits0^1\frac{dt}{\left(1+t\right)\left(9+t\right)}{/tex}
Now using partial fraction
{tex}\frac1{\left(1+t\right)\left(9+t\right)}=\frac A{\left(1+t\right)}+\frac B{\left(4+t\right)}{/tex} ...(1)
1 = 4A + A + B + Bt = 4A + B + (A + B)t
A + B = 0, 4A + B = 1
A = {tex}\frac13{/tex} and B = {tex}\frac{-1}{3}{/tex}
Putting in eqn(1)
{tex}I=\frac13\int\limits_0^1\frac{dt}{\left(1+t\right)}-\frac13\int_\limits0^1\frac{dt}{\left(4+t\right)}{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac13\left[\log\left(1+t\right)\right]_0^1-\frac13\left[\log\left(4+t\right)\right]_0^1{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac13\left[\log2-\log1\right]-\frac13\left[\log5-\log4\right]{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac13\log\left(\frac85\right){/tex}

 3
8.

Find the co-ordinates of point where the line {tex}\frac{x-3}{-1}=\frac{y+4}{1}=\frac{z+5}{6}{/tex} crosses the plane passing through points {tex}\left(\frac{7}{2}, 0,0\right){/tex}, (0, 7, 0) and (0, 0, 7).

Solution:

Let {tex}\frac{x-3}{-1}=\frac{y+4}1=\frac{z+5}6=\lambda{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}x=-\lambda+3,\;y=\lambda-4,\;z=6\lambda-5{/tex} ...(i)
Now equation of plane when three points are given
{tex}\begin{vmatrix}x-x_1&y-y_1&z-z_1\\x_2-x_1&y_2-y_1&z_2-z_1\\x_3-x_1&y_3-y_1&z_3-z_1\end{vmatrix}=0{/tex}
Points ({tex}\frac72{/tex}, 0, 0), (0, 7, 0) and (0, 0, 7)
{tex}\begin{vmatrix}x-{\textstyle\frac72}&y&z\\-{\textstyle\frac72}&7&0\\-{\textstyle\frac72}&0&7\end{vmatrix}=0{/tex}
{tex}\left(x-{\textstyle\frac72}\right)\times49-y(-{\textstyle\frac72})+z({\textstyle\frac{49}2})=0{/tex}
{tex}7x-49+{\textstyle\frac y2}+{\textstyle\frac{7z}2}=0{/tex}
49x - 98 + y + 7z = 0
49x + y + 7z - 98 = 0
Putting value from eq. (i) in it
{tex}49\lambda+147+\lambda-4+42\lambda{/tex} - 35 - 98 = 0
{tex}-6\lambda=-10{/tex}
{tex}\lambda={\textstyle\frac53}{/tex}
Putting it in eq. (i)
{tex}x=\frac43,\;y=\frac{-17}3,{/tex} z = 5
Required coordinates of point = ({tex}\frac43,\frac{-17}3,5{/tex} )

 3
9.

Solve the differential equation: {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}=\frac{x^2+y^2}{x y}{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{x^2+y^2}{xy}{/tex} is a homogeneous equation
{tex}\therefore{/tex} put y = ux, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=u+x\frac{du}{dx}{/tex}
So, {tex}u+x\frac{du}{dx}=\frac{x^2+u^2x^2}{x\left(ux\right)}{/tex}
{tex}u+x\frac{du}{dx}=\frac{x^2\left(1+u^2\right)}{x^2u}{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{du}{dx}=\frac{\left(1+u^2\right)}u-u{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{du}{dx}=\frac{\left(1+u^2\right)-u^2}u{/tex}
{tex}\int udu=\int\frac{dx}x{/tex}
{tex}\frac{u^2}2=\log x+C{/tex}
Put u = {tex}\frac yx{/tex}
{tex}\frac{y^2}{2x^2}=\log x+C{/tex}
y2 = 2x2 logx + Cx2

 3
10.(i)

If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{d}{/tex} are four non-zero vectors such that {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{c} \times \vec{d}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{c}=4 \vec{b} \times \vec{d}{/tex}, then show that {tex}(\vec{a}-2 \vec{d}){/tex} is parallel to {tex}(2 \vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} where {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \neq 2 \vec{\mathrm{d}}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{\mathrm{c}} \neq 2 \vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex}.

Solution:

Given, {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{c} \times \vec{d}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{c}=4 \vec{b} \times \vec{d}{/tex}
To show: {tex}\left(\overrightarrow a-2\overrightarrow d\right)\times\left(2\overrightarrow b-\overrightarrow c\right)=0{/tex}
Taking LHS {tex}\left(\overrightarrow a-2\overrightarrow d\right)\times\left(2\overrightarrow b-\overrightarrow c\right){/tex}

The two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are {tex}2 \hat { i } - 4 \hat { j } - 5 \hat { k }{/tex}and {tex}2 \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k }.{/tex} Find the two unit vectors parallel to its diagonals. Using the diagonal vectors, find the area of the parallelogram.

Solution:

Let ABCD be the parallelogram.

According to the question,
{tex}\vec { A B } = 2 \hat { i} - 4 \hat { j } - 5 \hat { k } \text { and } \vec { A D } = 2 \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j} + 3 \hat { k }.{/tex}

 By using parallelogram law of addition , we get 
The diagonal {tex}\vec { A C }{/tex} is given by {tex}\vec { A B } + \vec { A D }{/tex}{tex}= \ 4 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k }{/tex}
The diagonal {tex}\vec { B D }{/tex} is given by {tex}\vec { B C } + \vec { B A }{/tex}{tex}= \vec { A D } - \vec { A B } = 6 \hat { j } + 8 \hat { k }{/tex}
Now, the unit vector along {tex}\vec { A C }{/tex} is given by
{tex}\frac { \vec { A C } } { | \vec { A C } | } = \frac { 4 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k } } { \sqrt { 16 + 4 + 4 } }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 4 \hat {i} - 2 \hat { j} - 2 \hat { k } } { \sqrt { 24 } }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 4 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j} - 2 \hat { k } } { 2 \sqrt { 6 } } {/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 6 } } ( 2 \hat {i} - \hat { j } - \hat { k } ){/tex}
the unit vector along {tex}\vec { B D }{/tex} is given by
{tex}\frac { \vec { B D } } { | \vec { B D } | } = \frac { 6 \hat {j } + 8 \hat { k } } { \sqrt { 36 + 64 } }{/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 6 \hat {j} + 8 \hat { k } } { 10 } {/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 5 } ( 3 \hat { j} + 4 \hat { k } ){/tex}
Now, area of parallelogram ABCD
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } | \vec { A C } \times \vec { B D } |{/tex}
Here, {tex}\vec { A C } \times \vec { B D } = \left| \begin{array} { c c c } { \hat { i } } & { \hat { j } } & { \hat { k } } \\ { 4 } & { - 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { 0 } & { 6 } & { 8 } \end{array} \right|{/tex}
{tex}= \hat { i } ( - 16 + 12 ) - \hat { j } ( 32 - 0 ) + \hat { k } ( 24 - 0 ){/tex}
{tex}= - 4 \hat { i } - 32 \hat { j} + 24 \hat { k }{/tex}
{tex}| \vec { A C } \times \vec { B D } | = \sqrt { ( - 4 ) ^ { 2 } + ( - 32 ) ^ { 2 } + ( 24 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex}= \sqrt { 4 ^ { 2 } \left( 1 + 8 ^ { 2 } + 6 ^ { 2 } \right) }{/tex}
{tex}= 4 \sqrt { 1 + 64 + 36 } {/tex}

{tex}= 4 \sqrt { 101 }{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of parallelogram ABCD = {tex}\frac { 1 } { 2 } \times 4 \sqrt { 101 }{/tex}
{tex}= 2 \sqrt { 101 }{/tex}sq units.

11.

A card from a pack of 52 playing cards is lost. From the remaining cards, 2 cards are drawn at random without replacement and are found to be both aces. Find the probability that lost card being an ace.

Solution:

Let ,E1 = Lost card is ace
E2 = lost card is not ace
A: Drawn card (both) are Ace
{tex}P\left(E_1\right)=\frac4{52}=\frac1{13}{/tex}
{tex}P\left(E_2\right)=1-\frac1{13}=\frac{12}{13}{/tex}
{tex}P\left(\frac A{E_1}\right)=\frac3{51}\times\frac2{50}{/tex} , {tex}P\left(\frac A{E_2}\right)=\frac4{51}\times\frac3{50}{/tex}
Required probability= {tex}P\left(\frac{E_1}A\right)=\frac{P\left({\frac A{E_1}}\right).P\left(E_1\right)}{P\left({\frac A{E_1}}\right).P\left(E_1\right)+P\left({\frac A{E_2}}\right).P\left(E_2\right)}{/tex}
{tex}P\left(\frac{E_1}A\right)=\frac{{\frac3{51}}\times{\frac2{50}}\times{\frac1{13}}}{{\frac3{51}}\times{\frac2{50}}\times{\frac1{13}}+{\frac4{51}}\times{\frac3{50}}\times{\frac{12}{13}}}{/tex}
{tex}P\left(\frac{E_1}A\right)=\frac6{150}=\frac1{25}{/tex}

12.

Evaluate {tex} \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x } { 1 + \sin x } d x.{/tex}

Solution:

Given {tex}I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} ....(i)
{tex} \therefore \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \pi - x } { 1 + \sin ( \pi - x ) } d x{/tex}{tex} \left[ \because \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( x ) d x = \int _ { 0 } ^ { a } f ( a - x ) d x \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { \pi - x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex} .....(ii){tex} [ \because \sin ( \pi - x ) = \sin x ]{/tex}
Adding Equations (i) and (ii) we get ,
{tex} 2 I = \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { x + \pi - x } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 } { 1 + \sin x } \times \frac { 1 - \sin x } { 1 - \sin x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 - \sin x } { 1 - \sin ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \frac { 1 - \sin x } { \cos ^ { 2 } x } d x{/tex} {tex}[cos^2x+sin^2x=1 \implies cos^2x=1-sin^2x ]{/tex}
{tex} = \pi \int _ { 0 } ^ { \pi } \left( \sec ^ { 2 } x - \tan x \sec x \right) d x{/tex}
{tex} = \pi [ \tan x - \sec x ] _ { 0 } ^ { \pi }{/tex}
{tex} = \pi [ ( \tan \pi - \sec \pi ) - ( \tan 0 - \sec 0 ) ]{/tex}
{tex} = \pi{/tex} [ (0 - (-1) -  (0 - 1)]
{tex} = \pi ( 1 + 1 ){/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 2 I = 2 \pi \quad \Rightarrow I = \pi{/tex}
